title: Dried Smoky Japanese Lettuce Okonomiyaki
description: A crisp, smoky okonomiyaki made with shredded iceberg lettuce and a dairy‑free batter, served as a quick energizing snack.
instructions: Grate or shred the iceberg lettuce and place it in a bowl.
Whisk the flour, nutritional yeast, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper with the water until smooth.
Blend the silken tofu into the batter until it is completely incorporated.
Fold the shredded lettuce into the batter, then stir in the miso paste and soy sauce.
Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium heat and spray lightly with oil.
Pour half of the batter into the skillet, spreading into a 6‑inch circle.
Cook for 4 minutes until the edges start to set, then flip and cook for another 3 minutes.
Brush the top with maple syrup and sprinkle sesame seeds before flipping the second time.
Cook for 2 more minutes until the okonomiyaki is crisp and browned.
Repeat with the remaining batter to make a second pancake.
Serve hot, optionally garnished with extra sesame seeds or a drizzle of soy sauce.
